page: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server belonging to the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 208 vCPUs mapped to 104 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. This configuration is paired with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 11,400.0 GB (approximately 54.81 GB per physical core) and 8,389 GB of local storage, making it highly optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. While single-core performance is relatively modest—with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression/decompression tasks scoring in the bottom 25%—the massive core count drives top-tier multi-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core benchmark, Passmark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all rank in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 10,444.18 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 305.41 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, executing 159.37 million operations per second.

The hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: single-threaded execution speeds are average to weak, but parallelized processing and memory throughput are exceptionally strong. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a high-density resource allocation designed for workloads where massive RAM capacity is non-negotiable, avoiding the need to distribute databases across multiple smaller nodes. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational database management systems, massive data caching layers, and complex multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server in the MSv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with 104 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores (208 vCPUs) running at 2.7 GHz, paired with an expansive 11,400.0 GB of RAM and 8,389 GB of local storage. The server exhibits a distinct performance profile: while single-core benchmarks are relatively weak, its parallel processing capabilities are top-tier. Multi-core stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and memory bandwidth benchmarks all rank in the top 10%, with Redis SET operations reaching 159.37 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 84.24 GB/sec. This high-density resource configuration is highly efficient for consolidating massive databases, in-memory caching layers, and heavy multi-threaded enterprise workloads that demand extreme memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 208 vCPUs, 11,400 GB of RAM, and 8,389 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for massive databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 208 vCPUs, 11.4 TB RAM, and 8.3 TB local storage, built for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring over eleven terabytes of RAM and high multi-threaded processing power for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: Equipped with 208 vCPUs via 104 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores
Massive 11,400 GB RAM capacity for in-memory databases
Includes 8,389 GB of local storage for high-capacity data needs
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory read/write bandwidth
Optimized for large-scale database operations and Redis caching
